codeReview.summary: The local session backend contract is covered correctly in `agents-core`: `MemorySession` resolves configured replay limits before trimming history, and `SQLiteSession` returns the newest limited slice in chronological order while supporting `pop_item()` and `clear_session()`. The focused regressions in `crates/agents-core/src/memory/session.rs` and `crates/agents-core/src/memory/sqlite_session.rs` match VAL-INTEGRATIONS-001 and VAL-INTEGRATIONS-002, and I did not find correctness or coverage gaps within this feature’s stated scope.

sharedStateObservations.observation: `rust-runtime-worker` does not document a verification-only path for cases where a feature is already present on the current branch, even though this mission can assign such features. That forced the worker to formally deviate from the required test-first procedure despite doing the correct thing for an already-landed parity slice.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Skill procedure mandates "Write failing tests first" and "Implement only after the new/updated tests fail" (`.factory/skills/rust-runtime-worker/SKILL.md`:22-24). The reviewed handoff records `followedProcedure: false` and explicitly asks for a verification-only path when the feature has already landed (`/Users/staticpayload/.factory/missions/a79cf586-bde6-417e-92a4-dc7681913eac/handoffs/2026-04-16T15-27-40-122Z__session-backend-local-parity__e5dc30cf-7a8f-4e02-b207-965543a1812e.json`:99-100).

summary: Pass. The reviewed feature behavior is present and correctly exercised: `MemorySession` limit handling and `SQLiteSession` round-trip/pop/clear semantics align with the contract, with no feature-scope bugs found. I noted one skill-documentation gap around verification-only assignments for already-landed work.
